title: Listings
description: THE canonical, comparable record of one home — deduped across portals, normalized to true cost and real size, analyzed, located, and scored. Everything the app knows about a place hangs off this row.

dedupeKey.description: canonical identity computed by the intake dedupeKey() function (normalized address + rooms + size band + price band) — the same unit cross-posted on two portals merges into one row

url.description: canonical listing URL (the best of the merged duplicates)

portal.description: site(s) it was seen on, comma-joined, e.g. 'idealista, imovirtual'

priceAmount.description: the stated asking price — monthly rent (mode 'rent') or total price (mode 'buy')

trueCostMonthly.description: the all-in monthly cost the surveyor computed (rent + condo fees + utilities estimate; buy: mortgage estimate + charges). 0 = not yet computed

costBreakdown.description: array of { label, amount, basis:'stated'|'estimated', note } line items behind trueCostMonthly — every estimate is labelled, never silently blended

address.description: the address AS STATED by the listing (often vague — the real guess lives in location_guesses)

claimedLat.description: the portal's (often fuzzed) map pin latitude, if captured

claimedLng.description: the portal's (often fuzzed) map pin longitude, if captured

areaSqm.description: size in m² as stated by the listing

measuredAreaSqm.description: size the analyst re-derived (sum of per-room dimensions from the floor plan / listing text). 0 = not derivable

floor.description: floor / storey as stated, e.g. '3º, elevator'

description.description: the cleaned listing description, markdown, SANITIZED on ingest (captures are untrusted content)

photoUrls.description: array of photo/floor-plan objects as captured (with any caption text preserved alongside each: { url, caption })

flags.description: merged analysis flags, e.g. ['dated_kitchen','poor_light','size_overstated','photo_text_mismatch','fuzzed_pin'] — written by the scout analyst, rendered as chips on the feed

score.description: taste-ranked relevance 0..100 written by the ranker; higher = better match. 0 = not yet ranked

scoreSummary.description: md — WHY it scored what it did, citing the taste notes and hard constraints it hit/missed

dismissedReason.description: the user's stated reason on dismiss — the highest-value taste evidence

firstSeenAt.description: when the listing first entered the app

lastSeenAt.description: last time a capture or the refresh cron confirmed it live
